description: UI Spacing & Nav Color Consistency - Standardize Home page header styling across all tabs and align nav bar active text color with the pink-red icon color (#FF2D55) on both web and mobile apps.

userStories.title: Standardize Home page header on web
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Home page header to look the same as the Friends and Account page headers so the app feels polished and consistent.
acceptanceCriteria: In apps/web/src/app/(main)/page.tsx, change the 'Burn Buddies' header from <h2 className="m-0 text-lg font-semibold text-white"> to <h1 className="mb-6 text-2xl font-bold text-white"> to match Friends and Account headers
Home page container padding (pt-6 px-4) remains consistent with other pages
The header sits inside a flex row with a 'Log Workout' button — verify layout is not broken after changing from h2 to h1
Typecheck passes (cd apps/web && yarn typecheck)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Friends page header: <h1 className="text-2xl font-bold text-white"> with mb-6. Account page header: <h1 className="mb-6 text-2xl font-bold">. The Home header is currently in a flex row with a Log Workout button — be careful not to break that layout.

userStories.title: Update active nav bar text color on web
userStories.description: As a user, I want the active tab label in the navigation bar to use the same pink-red color as the tab icons so the nav feels cohesive.
acceptanceCriteria: In apps/web/src/components/NavBar.tsx, change the active link text class from text-primary to text-accent-pink in the desktop nav (desktopLinkClass variable, around line 57-58)
In apps/web/src/components/NavBar.tsx, change the active mobile nav label from text-primary to text-accent-pink (around line 100-101)
Inactive tab text colors remain unchanged — desktop: text-gray-400 hover:text-white, mobile: text-gray-500
The accent-pink color (#FF2D55) is already defined in globals.css and tailwind.config.ts — no new color definitions needed
Typecheck passes (cd apps/web && yarn typecheck)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Nav icons already use #FF2D55 (accent-pink) for active state. Active text currently uses text-primary (#FF9500 orange). The Tailwind class text-accent-pink maps to #FF2D55 via the existing theme.

userStories.title: Standardize Home screen header on mobile
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Home screen header on the mobile app to have consistent styling and text with the Friends and Account screens.
acceptanceCriteria: In apps/mobile/src/screens/HomeScreen.tsx, change the header text from 'buddyburn 🔥' to 'Burn Buddies' (around line 334)
In apps/mobile/src/screens/HomeScreen.tsx, change headerTitle.color from '#E05A00' to '#333' to match the Friends screen header color (around line 633)
All three screen headers (Home, Friends, Account) use fontSize: 22 and fontWeight: 'bold' — verify Home matches
No layout shifts or visual regressions on the Home screen
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Friends headerTitle color is #333, Account title color is #111827. Use #333 to match Friends. Header padding is already consistent (paddingTop: 56, paddingBottom: 12, paddingHorizontal: 16).

userStories.title: Update active tab bar text color on mobile
userStories.description: As a user, I want the active tab label in the mobile app's tab bar to use the pink-red accent color, matching the web app's nav bar.
acceptanceCriteria: In apps/mobile/App.tsx, change activeTabText.color from '#E05A00' to '#FF2D55' in tabStyles (around line 171-173)
Inactive tab text color remains '#9ca3af' (gray) — do not change tabStyles.tabText
Tab bar is still readable and visually clear with the new color
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The mobile tab bar uses emoji icons (🏠 👥 👤) not SVG icons, so there is no separate icon color to coordinate with. The #FF2D55 color matches what the web nav icons use.
